chem-ammonia-pyramid: Create a large clean ammonia NH3 trigonal pyramidal diagram. Put N at (0,0), H atoms at (-1.6,-1), (1.6,-1), and (0,1.5), lone pair as two dots above N at y=0.55, and bond angle 107 degrees to the right. Keep labels away from bonds; no overlapping text.
chem-buffer-diagram: Create a clean buffer reaction diagram with two rows, not a beaker: top row H+ + A- -> HA, bottom row OH- + HA -> A- + H2O. Put each reaction in a separate horizontal lane with arrows, labels acid added and base added at left, and no overlapping text.
chem-redox-half-reactions: Create a clean redox electron transfer diagram: Zn atom at left, Cu2+ ion at right, one arrow labeled 2e- from Zn to Cu2+, products Zn2+ below left and Cu below right, labels oxidation near Zn and reduction near Cu. Keep text separated.
chem-titration-curve-weak-acid: Create a large weak acid strong base titration curve. Axes: Volume NaOH on x, pH on y. Draw one smooth S-curve, label buffer region left, half-equivalence in middle-left, equivalence point above pH 7, and endpoint upper right. Use only ASCII labels and no tiny annotations.
chem-born-haber: Create a readable simplified Born-Haber cycle for NaCl with large spacing: Na(s)+1/2Cl2(g) at bottom left, gaseous ions Na+(g)+Cl-(g) at top center, NaCl(s) at bottom right. Draw arrows labeled sublimation, ionization, dissociation, electron affinity, lattice enthalpy. Use only five arrows.
chem-vsepr-water: Create a clean water H2O VSEPR diagram: O at center, H atoms down-left and down-right, two lone pairs as dot pairs above O, 104.5 degree angle arc below O, labels outside. Avoid overlapping LP and angle text.
math-probability-tree: Create a spacious probability tree: start node at left, first split to Rain 0.3 and No rain 0.7, second split from each to Late and On time. Put terminal probabilities at far right in separate vertical lanes. Use short labels and no overlapping equations.
math-box-plot: Create a large box-and-whisker plot on a number line from 0 to 15: min 2, Q1 5, median 7, Q3 10, max 14. Draw one box, whiskers, and labels above the five values with enough spacing.
math-inscribed-angle: Create a clean circle theorem diagram: circle center O, chord AB near bottom, point C on upper circumference, central angle AOB and inscribed angle ACB marked with small arcs, labels outside, no text inside the angle arcs.
math-exponential-growth: Create a clean graph of y=2^x: axes, curve from x=-2 to x=3, points (0,1), (1,2), (2,4), horizontal asymptote y=0 dashed, label curve away from points. Keep graph large and uncluttered.
physics-simple-pendulum: Create a clean simple pendulum diagram: pivot at (0,0), string to bob at (2,-3), dashed vertical from pivot, angle theta near pivot, tension T along string, weight mg downward from bob, component mg sin theta tangent to arc. Keep labels outside arrows.
physics-torque-wrench: Create a clean torque wrench diagram: pivot O at left, wrench as thick line to upper right, force F applied at handle end at angle, lever arm r along wrench, perpendicular distance r sin theta as dashed segment, torque tau curved arrow near pivot. Keep labels separated.
physics-two-block-pulley-table: Create a clean two-block pulley diagram: m1 on table left, pulley at table edge right, m2 hanging, string over pulley, N and m1g on m1, T on string, f left on m1, m2g down on m2, a down on m2. Leave whitespace between force labels.
physics-solenoid-field: Create a readable solenoid magnetic field diagram: draw five coil loops around a horizontal axis, current arrows on two turns, B field arrows straight through the center, N pole on right and S pole on left, and a few faint outside return field lines. Keep labels sparse.
physics-rolling-cylinder: Create a rolling cylinder diagram: cylinder on horizontal ground, center O, radius R to contact point, velocity v of center to right, angular velocity omega curved arrow, friction f at contact point, and label v=omega R below. Keep labels outside the circle.
